title: Free Zoom Alternatives for Linux

proprietary.name: Zoom
proprietary.description: A widely used video conferencing platform known for its ease of use, reliable performance, and features like breakout rooms, virtual backgrounds, and cloud recording. Popular for both business meetings and personal calls.

alternatives.name: Jitsi Meet
alternatives.description: A fully open-source video conferencing solution that requires no account or download to join a meeting. Jitsi Meet supports high-quality video and audio, screen sharing, and can be self-hosted for complete privacy. It is the most accessible open-source alternative to Zoom, with public instances available for immediate use.
features: No account or installation required to join meetings
End-to-end encryption support for secure calls
Self-hostable for full control over infrastructure
Screen sharing with presenter mode and remote control
Built-in chat, polls, and hand-raise features
Live streaming integration with YouTube and others

alternatives.name: BigBlueButton
alternatives.description: An open-source web conferencing system designed specifically for online learning and education. BigBlueButton provides a virtual classroom experience with an integrated whiteboard, breakout rooms, shared notes, and polling tools. It is widely used by universities and schools around the world.
features: Multi-user interactive whiteboard with annotation tools
Breakout rooms for small group collaboration
Shared notes and real-time collaborative editing
LMS integration with Moodle, Canvas, and Sakai

alternatives.name: Element
alternatives.description: A decentralized communication platform built on the Matrix protocol, offering end-to-end encrypted messaging, voice calls, and video conferencing. Element can be self-hosted and federated, meaning different organizations can run their own servers while still communicating with each other seamlessly.
features: End-to-end encryption for all communication by default
Decentralized and federated via the Matrix protocol
Group video calls with screen sharing support
Bridging to Slack, Discord, IRC, and other platforms
Persistent chat rooms alongside voice and video

verdict: Jitsi Meet is the best Zoom alternative for Linux users seeking a frictionless video conferencing experience -- it requires no accounts, runs in any browser, and can be self-hosted for maximum privacy. BigBlueButton is the top choice for educators needing virtual classroom features like whiteboards, breakout rooms, and LMS integration. Element with Matrix is ideal for organizations that want a permanent, decentralized communication platform combining persistent chat with voice and video calls.

seo.title: Free Zoom Alternatives for Linux in 2026 | Open-Source Video Conferencing
seo.description: Discover the best free and open-source Zoom alternatives for Linux. Compare Jitsi Meet, BigBlueButton, and Element for secure, self-hosted video conferencing and communication.
